title: Sidebar Panel
subtitle: Sliding side panels with CDK Overlay to display dynamic content

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.description: Open a side panel from the SidebarPanelService. The panel slides from the screen edge with a smooth animation.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Allows defining the size with which the panel will open.
sizes.note: You can use predefined values such as 'xs', 'sm', 'md', 'lg', 'xl' and 'full'. On mobile, the 'full' size will be applied automatically to ensure an optimal experience on mobile devices.

positions.title: Positions
positions.description: Control from which side of the screen the panel slides.
positions.note: Use the position property to define the side from which the panel will open using 'right', 'left', 'top' or 'bottom' as values.

html-content.title: Custom HTML content
html-content.description: Render arbitrary HTML content inside the panel using Angular templates.
html-content.note: Use the htmlContent property to pass an HTML string. You can generate the HTML dynamically or concatenate it with your data. This option does not require creating a full Angular component.

template-content.title: Custom template content (TemplateRef)
template-content.description: Use Angular templates with context data and functions without needing a full component. Ideal for reusable content with dynamic data.
template-content.note: Use the contentTemplate property to pass a TemplateRef and templateContext to pass data and functions. The template receives the context via let-varname variables. This allows interactivity without creating a component.

dynamic-component.title: Dynamic component
dynamic-component.description: Load any Angular component inside the panel and pass data via dependency injection
dynamic-component.note: Use the data property to pass data to the dynamically loaded component. This data is injected using the SIDEBAR_PANEL_DATA token. If we also want to reference the panel itself from the loaded component, we can inject SIDEBAR_PANEL_REF.

dynamic-component-events.title: Dynamic component @Outputs events
dynamic-component-events.description: Listen to events emitted by the component loaded inside the panel to handle interactions and close the panel when necessary.
dynamic-component-events.note: You can subscribe to the outputs of the dynamically loaded component. Use panelRef.componentInstance to access the child component instance and subscribe to its @Output().

minimizable.title: Minimizable panel
minimizable.description: Panels can be minimized to a chip in the NuiDock, the unified dock at the bottom of the screen, to free up screen space while preserving state, and can be restored when desired.
minimizable.note:
Use the minimizable attribute to make the Sidebar minimizable. Additionally, you can use the minimize() method of SidebarPanel to minimize the panel programmatically and restore() to restore it. If minimizable is enabled, the id attribute is required to identify the minimized panel. You can also check if the Sidebar is minimized via isMinimized.

Dock chip customization: Use dockTabConfig to customize the icon, title or CSS classes of the chip shown in the bottom dock. If no icon is specified, a directional indicator is shown automatically based on the panel position.

close-button.title: Close button
close-button.description: Control the visibility of the close button in the panel header.
close-button.note: Use the showCloseButton property to show or hide the close button in the panel header.

backdrop.title: Backdrop
backdrop.description: Adds a dark background behind the panel to create a modal-like experience. Control whether clicking the backdrop closes the panel.
backdrop.note: Use the hasBackdrop property to enable/disable the backdrop (default true) and closeOnBackdropClick to control whether clicking the backdrop closes the panel.

custom-backdrop.title: Custom backdrop
custom-backdrop.description: Customize the backdrop with custom CSS classes. You can apply effects such as blur, custom colors and transitions.
custom-backdrop.note: Use the backdropClass property to add custom CSS classes to the panel backdrop. You can add a single class or an array of classes.

multiple.title: Multiple panels
multiple.description: Open multiple panels simultaneously from different sides. Control stacking with z-index and close all at once.
multiple.note: Each open panel is independent and can have its own configuration. Use the closeAll() method of the SidebarPanelService to programmatically close all open panels simultaneously.

footer-actions.title: Footer actions
footer-actions.description: Add action buttons in the panel footer.
footer-actions.note:
Use customButtons for simple actions or footerTemplate for fully customized layouts. You can combine multiple buttons with the different NUI variants and colors.

To use loading or disabled reactively, you can inject the SidebarPanelActionsService into your child component to control the button states from within the component, use the nuiSidebarPanelFooter directive on your custom buttons to link them to predefined actions like 'confirm' or 'cancel', or emit custom events and subscribe to them from the child component or using an ng-template in the parent component, without the directive.

child-footer-actions.title: Actions from child component
child-footer-actions.description: The child component can register its own footer actions using SidebarPanelActionsService.
child-footer-actions.note: This approach is ideal for forms where the child handles all the logic (validation, saving, states). The child component injects SidebarPanelActionsService and registers actions via register(). Actions can have reactive states (disabled, loading) and execute complex logic.
child-footer-actions.note2: Alternatively, use the nuiSidebarPanelFooter directive inside a dynamic component for full footer control with native HTML. Unlike SidebarPanelActionsService, which registers button data rendered by the shell, the directive projects a complete ng-template: any Angular binding re-evaluates on every change detection cycle without calling update().

prevent-close.title: Prevent close (dirty guard)
prevent-close.description: Block the panel from closing when there are unsaved changes or when an async condition is not met. Works for all close triggers: backdrop click, Escape key and the header close button.
prevent-close.note: Pass a synchronous or async function to preventClose. Return true to allow the close, false to block it. Use panelRef.closePrevented() to react every time a close is blocked — useful to show a toast or shake the panel.

update-ref.title: Update panel in flight
update-ref.description: Modify the panel title, header or footer template after it has been opened, without closing and reopening it. Useful when async data loads or the panel state changes.
update-ref.note: Call panelRef.updateTitle(), panelRef.updateHeaderTemplate() or panelRef.updateFooterTemplate() at any point after open(). The shell re-renders automatically thanks to markForCheck() being called internally.

service.title: SidebarPanelService
service.description: Inject SidebarPanelService to open and manage sidebar panels. All methods return a SidebarPanelRef.

rows.open: Opens a panel with a dynamic component.
rows.close: Closes a specific panel by ID.
rows.getPanel: Returns the panel stack item for the given ID, or undefined if not found.
rows.getAllPanels: Returns an array of all currently open panel stack items.
rows.openPanelsCount: Getter returning the number of currently open panels.

ref.title: SidebarPanelRef — Properties
ref.description: Read-only properties exposed by the reference returned from open().

rows.id: Unique identifier of the panel instance.
rows.componentInstance: Reference to the dynamically loaded component instance, or null if not yet rendered.
rows.config: The resolved configuration object for this panel.
rows.state: The current state of the panel (SidebarPanelState).
rows.isOpen: true if the panel is open or in the process of opening.
rows.isClosed: true if the panel is closed or in the process of closing.
rows.isMinimized: true if the panel is currently minimized.
rows.stateHistory: Read-only array recording the sequence of states the panel has transitioned through.
rows.close: Closes the panel programmatically. Resolves when the closing animation completes.
rows.minimize: Minimizes the panel to the dock. Only available when minimizable: true is set in the config.
rows.restore: Restores the panel from its minimized state.
rows.updateTitle: Updates the panel title after it has been opened.
rows.updateHeaderTemplate: Replaces the panel header with a custom TemplateRef after opening.
rows.updateFooterTemplate: Replaces the panel footer with a custom TemplateRef after opening.
rows.canTransitionTo: Returns true if the panel can transition to the given state from its current state.
rows.afterOpened: Observable that emits once when the panel has fully opened.
rows.afterClosed: Observable that emits once when the panel has closed, carrying the optional result value.
rows.backdropClick: Observable that emits on every click on the backdrop overlay.
rows.keydownEvents: Observable that emits keyboard events originating from within the panel.
rows.stateChanged: Observable that emits each time the panel transitions to a new state.
rows.closePrevented: Observable that emits when a close attempt is blocked by the preventClose guard.

ref-methods.title: SidebarPanelRef — Methods
ref-methods.description: Methods available on the reference returned by open(). Use them to control the panel lifecycle, update content, and observe events.

tokens.title: Injection tokens
tokens.description: Use these tokens to inject the sidebar panel context inside a dynamic component loaded by the service.

rows.SIDEBAR_PANEL_DATA: Inject the typed data passed via the data config property into the dynamic component.
rows.SIDEBAR_PANEL_REF: Inject the SidebarPanelRef instance inside the shell or dynamic component.
rows.SIDEBAR_PANEL_CONFIG: Inject the full, read-only configuration object of the current panel.

config.title: SidebarPanelConfig
config.description: Configuration object passed to open(). Properties are merged with the global values from provideNUI().

rows.id: Unique panel identifier. Required when minimizable: true.
rows.position: Edge of the viewport from which the panel slides in.
rows.size: Predefined panel size applied via a CSS variable.
rows.width: Custom CSS width for left/right panels. Overrides size.
rows.height: Custom CSS height for top/bottom panels. Overrides size.
rows.maxWidth: Maximum CSS width for the panel. Useful to prevent overly wide panels on large screens.
rows.maxHeight: Maximum CSS height for the panel. Useful to prevent overly tall panels.
rows.data: Arbitrary data injected into the dynamic component via SIDEBAR_PANEL_DATA.
rows.contentTemplate: Angular TemplateRef rendered as the panel body.
rows.htmlContent: Raw HTML string rendered as the panel body. Sanitized by Angular's DomSanitizer.
rows.title: Title displayed in the panel header.
rows.showHeader: Whether the panel header bar is rendered.
rows.showCloseButton: Whether the close button is shown in the panel header.
rows.headerTemplate: Custom TemplateRef that replaces the default panel header.
rows.footerTemplate: Custom TemplateRef rendered in the panel footer area.
rows.customButtons: Inline footer button definitions. Evaluated once at open time.
rows.hasBackdrop: Whether a backdrop overlay is rendered behind the panel.
rows.backdropClass: One or more CSS classes applied to the backdrop element.
rows.closeOnBackdropClick: Whether clicking the backdrop closes the panel.
rows.closeOnEscape: Whether pressing the Escape key closes the panel.
rows.closeOnRouteChange: Whether the panel closes automatically when the route changes.
rows.preventClose: Guard function called before each close attempt. Return false to block.
rows.autoFocus: Whether focus moves into the panel on open. Accepts true, false, or a CSS selector.
rows.mobileFullScreen: Whether the panel expands to full screen on mobile viewports.
rows.panelClass: One or more CSS classes applied to the panel host element.
rows.scrollStrategy: Strategy for handling page scroll while the panel is open.
rows.animationDuration: Duration of the open and close animations in milliseconds.
rows.zIndex: Base z-index value for the panel and its backdrop.
rows.minimizable: Whether the panel can be minimized to the dock.
rows.dockTabConfig: Customization options for the dock chip shown when the panel is minimized.
rows.allowMultiple: Whether more than one panel instance can be open simultaneously.
rows.lazyLoad: Whether the panel content is rendered lazily on first open.
rows.ariaLabel: Value of the aria-label attribute on the panel root element.
rows.ariaDescribedBy: ID of an element that provides an accessible description for the panel.

custom-button.title: SidebarPanelCustomButton
custom-button.description: Inline button definition passed through customButtons in the configuration. It is evaluated only once when the modal opens — use SidebarPanelActionsService for reactive states.

rows.prefixIcon: Remix Icon class displayed before the button label (e.g., ri-save-line).
rows.suffixIcon: Remix Icon class displayed after the button label (e.g., ri-arrow-right-line).
rows.color: NUI color applied to the button.
rows.variant: Button variant: solid, outline, or ghost.
rows.size: NUI size applied to the button.
rows.callback: Click handler invoked with the SidebarPanelRef. Required.
rows.disabled: Whether the button is rendered in a disabled state.
rows.loading: Whether the button shows a loading spinner.
rows.loadingPosition: Position of the loading spinner relative to the label.
rows.class: One or more additional CSS classes applied to the button element.
rows.width: Button width mode: auto (content-sized) or full (full container width).

minimized-tab.title: DockTabConfig
minimized-tab.description: Customization of the dock chip when the panel is minimized. Properties map to the NuiDockItem managed by NuiDockService.

rows.prefixIcon: Remix Icon class for the prefix icon displayed inside the dock chip.
rows.suffixIcon: Remix Icon class for the suffix icon. Only shown when prefixIcon is set. Useful for indicating additional status such as a notification dot.
rows.hideSuffixIcon: When true, hides the directional arrow icon that indicates the sidebar panel's original position.
rows.label: Title override for the dock chip. Falls back to the panel title if not set.
rows.cssClass: One or more additional CSS classes applied to the dock chip button.

footer-actions.title: SidebarPanelAction
footer-actions.description: Definition of actions registered from child components for the panel footer

rows.id: Optional unique identifier for the action. Useful for targeted updates via updateById().
rows.label: Button label. Required.
rows.prefixIcon: Remix Icon class displayed before the button label (e.g., ri-save-line).
rows.suffixIcon: Remix Icon class displayed after the button label (e.g., ri-arrow-right-line).
rows.color: NUI color applied to the button.
rows.variant: Button variant: solid, outline, or ghost.
rows.size: NUI size applied to the button.
rows.disabled: Whether the button is rendered in a disabled state.
rows.loading: Whether the button shows a loading spinner.
rows.loadingPosition: Position of the loading spinner relative to the label.
rows.class: One or more additional CSS classes applied to the button element.
rows.width: Button width mode: auto (content-sized) or full (full container width).

footer-actions-service.title: SidebarPanelActionsService
footer-actions-service.description: Service that allows child components to register actions in the panel footer

rows.register: Registers a complete set of footer actions, replacing any previously registered ones.
rows.add: Appends a single action to the existing set of footer actions.
rows.update: Partially updates the action at the given index.
rows.updateById: Partially updates the action matching the given ID.
rows.setDisabled: Sets the disabled state of the action at the given index.
rows.setLoading: Sets the loading state of the action at the given index.

footer-directive.title: SidebarPanelFooterDirective
footer-directive.description: Directive applied to an ng-template inside a dynamic component. It projects the entire template into the panel footer with native Angular bindings.

colors.title: Color variables
colors.description: CSS variables to control the colors of the side panel: background, text, borders and overlay. These variables automatically adapt to the light/dark mode of the active theme.

bg.description: Panel background color. Adapts dynamically based on the active theme.

border.description: Panel border color. Adapts dynamically based on the active theme.

layout.title: Layout variables
layout.description: CSS variables to control the size and spacing of the side panel: predefined size presets (xs to full) and header and content padding

typography.title: Typography variables
typography.description: CSS variables to control the typography of the side panel title

elevation.title: Elevation variables
elevation.description: CSS variables to control the shadow and z-index of the side panel and overlay

scrollbar.title: Scrollbar variables
scrollbar.description: CSS variables to control the appearance of the side panel scrollbar

scrollThumbHoverBg.description: Scrollbar thumb background color on hover.

examples.title: Customization examples
examples.description: SCSS examples to customize side panel styles in your application

tokens.title: Translation tokens
tokens.description: The SidebarPanel component uses generic NuiI18nService tokens for the close and minimize control buttons.
tokens.note: All tokens are shared between multiple components. Override them globally with provideNuiI18n({...}) in your app.config.ts.

close.description: Accessible label for the close button in the panel header.

minimize.description: Accessible label for the minimize button in the panel header.

restore.description: Accessible label for the restore button, visible when the panel is minimized.

expand.description: Accessible label for the expand button.

collapse.description: Accessible label for the collapse button.

roles.title: ARIA roles and attributes
roles.description: The SidebarPanel implements an accessible dialog-like pattern so screen readers recognize it as a distinct content area.

role.description: The panel root carries role="dialog" to signal a distinct region to assistive technologies.

labelledby.description: aria-labelledby points to the panel title element, giving the dialog an accessible name derived from the visible heading.

closeLabel.description: The close button uses the i18n token close as aria-label.

minimizeLabel.description: The minimize/restore button exposes the corresponding i18n token (minimize or restore) as aria-label according to state.

expandLabel.description: The expand/collapse button exposes the corresponding token (expand or collapse) as aria-label.

backdrop.description: The background overlay is hidden from assistive technologies with aria-hidden="true".

naming.title: Accessible name
naming.description: The accessible name of the panel is obtained from its visible title via aria-labelledby. Control buttons are labelled with i18n tokens.
naming.note: Always provide a visible title inside the panel, as it automatically becomes the dialog name.

title.description: The visible panel title provides the accessible name of the role="dialog" region.

buttons.description: Control buttons (close, minimize, expand) use i18n tokens as accessible labels.

keyboard.title: Keyboard interaction
keyboard.description: The SidebarPanel supports keyboard navigation for all interactive controls.

escape.description: Closes the panel in modal mode, returning focus to the element that opened it.

tab.description: Cycles focus between the panel's interactive elements. Focus is trapped while the panel is open in modal mode.

enterSpace.description: Activates the focused control button (close, minimize, expand).

setup.title: Global configuration
setup.description: Set default values for all SidebarPanel instances in your application using the provideNUI() function. Any property defined here is automatically applied to every panel opened via SidebarPanelService that does not override it at call time.
setup.note: Only the properties you want to override need to be defined. Properties not specified fall back to the library defaults.

defaults.title: Configurable properties
defaults.description: Full list of configurable properties through SidebarPanelConfig, their types and the library defaults.

rows.position: Panel position relative to the viewport (left, right, top or bottom)
rows.size: Panel size — maps to a predefined width or height CSS variable
rows.showHeader: Whether the panel header bar is visible by default
rows.showCloseButton: Whether a close button is shown in the panel header
rows.hasBackdrop: Whether a backdrop overlay is rendered behind the panel
rows.closeOnBackdropClick: Whether clicking the backdrop closes the panel
rows.closeOnEscape: Whether pressing Escape closes the panel
rows.closeOnRouteChange: Whether the panel closes automatically on navigation
rows.autoFocus: Whether focus moves into the panel on open (true, false or a CSS selector)
rows.mobileFullScreen: Whether the panel occupies the full screen on mobile viewports
rows.scrollStrategy: Strategy used to handle page scroll while the panel is open
rows.ariaLabel: Value of the aria-label attribute on the panel root element
rows.animationDuration: Panel open/close animation duration in milliseconds
rows.minimizable: Whether panels can be minimised (requires a unique id per instance)
rows.allowMultiple: Whether more than one panel can be open at the same time
rows.lazyLoad: Whether panel content is rendered lazily on first open

priority.title: Resolution priority
priority.description: NUI resolves configuration values using the following priority chain (highest wins):
